logistics.lodging_strategy: Abundant wineries, B&Bs, and hotels in Clare town; book early for race weekend.

final_verdict.one_liner: Premier gran fondo blending Clare Valley's wine country beauty with solid road challenge.

youtube_data.search_query: Clare Classic gran fondo cycling Clare Valley

videos.title: Clare Classic - Sunday, 5 April 2020
videos.description:
The Clare Classic returns to the beautiful Clare Valley of South Australia on the weekend 3-5 April, 2020.

160km-110km-50km courses available.

Register now - www.clareclassic.com.au

videos.title: 2018 Clare Classic Post Event Video
videos.description:
Catch all the highlights from the inaugural 2018 Clare Classic cycling gran fondo through the beautiful Clare Valley region of South Australia.
For more information on upcoming events, visit: www.clareclassic.com.au

videos.title: SKODA Ring of Clare Cycle 2017
videos.description: SKODA Ring of Clare Cycle 2017 (formerly Granfondo Hibernia) - Starting and finishing in Ennis on Saturday 29th July 2017 - choice of 160km and 120km routes taking you by the Clare coast, loop head and Shannon estuary

videos.title: ŠKODA Ring of Clare 2017
videos.description: Over 2,000 cyclists took part in the Ring of Clare Cycle​ on Saturday July 29th which marked the second event in the 2017 ŠKODA Celtic Series. The next ŠKODA Celtic Series event rolls into Drogheda on September 10th for the Tour de Boyne Valley, check out http://celticseries.ie/ for additional information.

videos.title: ŠKODA Boyne Valley Classic 2016
videos.description:
Over 500 cyclists took part in the ŠKODA Boyne Valley Classic on Sunday September 18th 2016. Irish cycling legend Stephen Roche led the group, for this, the final event of the inaugural ‘ŠKODA Celtic Cycle Series 2016’. The Drogheda event brings the total number of cyclists that took to the saddle for ŠKODA’s cycling events this year to over 4,000. 

The Tour de Conamara and the ŠKODA Granfondo Hibernia in Ennis, which both took place earlier this year, made up the trio of the events for 2016.

key_challenges.name: First 80k headwind section
key_challenges.description: Really tough into a headwind for the first 80k, but once you turn the pace goes through the roof.

key_challenges.name: Clare hills
key_challenges.description: There's a lot of hills in Clare according to riders.

terrain_notes.text: Coastal route with significant hills throughout Clare. The return section after turning from the headwind feels like heaven and earth, described as being on the edge of the world.

race_day_tips.text: Expect a tough first 80k into headwind, but pace will increase significantly once you turn and have the wind at your back.

additional_quotes.text: We have 2,000 people taking part tomorrow so like this tomorrow I have an option of two routes a 120 km route and a 160 km route
Really really really well organized it's good good tough one out tough one out all the way to the coast
The family atmosphere and the warmth of the crowd and the all the kind of people that rocked up for it it was absolutely amazing

rider_intel.search_text: The Ring of Clare cycle features two distance options: 120km and 160km routes with 2,000 participants. Riders describe it as a coastal route with significant hills throughout Clare Valley. The major challenge is the first 80k into a tough headwind, but once riders turn, the pace increases dramatically with tailwind assistance. The terrain takes riders all the way to the coast, with the return section described as feeling like being on the edge of the world. Weather conditions can include headwinds and occasional rain. The event is noted as very well organized with good marshalling, food stations, and support throughout. Riders emphasize the challenging hill sections and the dramatic difference between the outbound headwind section and the return journey with favorable winds.
